summary: Enum auto complete not consistently functional

description.text:
### Tested versions

Present in all of Godot 4.

### System information

Windows 10 - 4.5 Dev 4

### Issue description

When using an Enum in gdscript, the auto-complete for the enum values doesn't show up with match statement, at least no consistently. Given the following code:
`
enum state {IDLE, WALK, RUN}

func whatever(my_state: state):
        if my_state == state.WALK:
               do_stuff()

func lorem(my_state: state):
       match my_state:
              state.WALK:
                    do_some_more()
`
When typing out the if statement, WALK along with RUN and IDLE will show up in the auto completion. However, when typing the match statement, no such auto complete/hints show up. Stranger more, finishing typing out the match statement and then going back to the pattern, deleting WALK, only then will WALK, RUN, IDLE show up in the auto-complete.

summary: Bone rotates "the wrong way" during animation transition interpolation.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in:
v4.4.1.stable.flathub [49a5bc7b6]
v4.4.1 MacOS arm64

### System information

Linux Desktop (AMD Ryzen 7 5700X3D, AMD Radeon 6700XT)  running Fedora Workstation 42, Macbook Pro M1 Pro Max 14" running MacOS Sequoia

### Issue description

While I was working on a 3D character I noticed certain animation transitions could cause one of the bones of the rig to rotate "the other way around". 

To reproduce the issue, I created the scene with a `CharacterBody3D` that can jump, fall and remain still.  The fall state is entered when the character stops going up or when the jump button is released. If that is the case, the playing  `jump`  animation transitions to the `fall`  one halfway, making the character weapon to flip around:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7ada16c2-c786-4818-9028-16f380d3d457

Both animations were made in Blender. The keyframes for the bone in question use Quaternions work fine in Blender. If I convert said keyframes from Quaternion to Euler angles in Blender, a similar issue to what is seen in the video appears. However, fixing it and exporting the asset again does not solve the problem in Godot.

I am not exactly sure whether this just me missing something, or a bug, a missing setting in the animation import window in Godot (which would allow the rotation track to be imported as Euler/Quaternions)

Please, do not hesitate on asking anything. I might have missed something while writing this. Thanks in advance.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce:
1 - Open the MRP and play  `main.tscn`
2 - Press and hold `left mouse button` or `space` to jump:
3 - Release the button halfway through the jump to trigger the animation transition from `jump` to `fall`, which displays the bug


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[animation-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20542223/animation-bug.zip)

summary: Add warning when system font fallback is used.

description.text: Adds warning displayed when system fallback font is used, warning can be disabled from the project settings, only enabled for debug builds, and by default is displayed only when running from editor with attached debugger. It is not displayed when system fonts are used directly (using `SystemFont` class). Warning include used system font and text string that triggered it (without duplication): System font fallback is useful, but is a constant source of confusion, since different platforms have different fallback capabilities and sets of available fonts. See following issues for example, there are multiple other similar issues: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/106824 https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/84590 https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/78921 https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/77903

summary: Texture format error on export: Show project setting

description.text:
Changing the project setting manually will prompt for a restart, which will trigger the required re-import of textures.

Previously the project setting would be changed automatically but textures would not be re-imported, which could be unexpected (would lead to exports with missing textures).

New behavior:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a32d852b-19b3-4c1f-a877-26671725faa1

- Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/106992

Advantages over changing the setting automatically and showing a restart dialog:

- Less magic, makes clear that there's a *Project* Setting and where it's located.
- Allows the user to hover the project setting for more documentation.
- The user could understand that they would probably want to turn the project setting back off if they ever decide to uncheck the checkbox in the export settings.

summary: Xbox Controller Not Recognized in Godot 4.x on macOS (M1, Sequoia 15.5), but Works in Godot 3.6

description.text:
### Tested versions

✅ 3.6.0: Controller recognized
❌ 4.2.2 (stable): Controller NOT recognized
❌ 4.4.1 (latest from godotengine.org): Controller NOT recognized

### System information

macOS Sequoia 15.5 MacBook Air (M1 chip, Apple Silicon)

### Issue description

In Godot 4.x, Input.get_connected_joypads() returns an empty array [] even when an Xbox controller is connected (via both USB and Bluetooth).
In Godot 3.6, the same controller is recognized and returns [0].

This suggests a regression or change in how Godot 4.x initializes SDL2 input on macOS (potentially related to the input backend or entitlements/sandboxing on Apple Silicon).

Xbox Wireless Controller (tried both Bluetooth and USB-C)
Controller works correctly in Other apps (Steam, hardwaretester.com/gamepad, etc.)

### Steps to reproduce

Use macOS 15.5 Sequoia on an M1 Mac.
Connect an Xbox controller via USB or Bluetooth.
Create a new Godot 4.x project and attach this script to any node:

func _ready():
	print("Connected joypads: ", Input.get_connected_joypads())

Run the scene.

Expected:
Console should show [0] from get_connected_joypads().

Actual:
get_connected_joypads() returns []
Same controller and script is detected normally in Godot 3.6


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[controller-testing.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20536358/controller-testing.zip)

summary: Project -> export -> exclude_filter not working

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible: 4.4.1

### System information

MacOS

### Issue description

In the project export, a filter file was set with the contents 「\*.md,*.txt」

After successfully exporting the Android APK, it still includes the specified file extensions.

It has been confirmed that the setting exclude_filter="\*.md,*.txt" appears in the export_presets.cfg file.

### Steps to reproduce

### Issue description

I create a **scene** with a root node that has an **export variable of type Node**, and then set some node in that same scene to be its default value in the **editor.** 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/36b3554d-a67e-4431-9912-823fe5e86a0b)

Upon instantiating this scene in another scene, and trying to set that export variable to Null (with the _Clear_ option in the editor) and running the project, Godot undoes that change and sets it to the previous default.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/01764c16-b0b9-4f07-8f46-62bcef7d2ddf)

### Steps to reproduce

I attached an **MRP**. To use it, go to the **main** scene. In it, access the Node2D child node instance. In the editor, modify its Child export variable to null using "**Clear**". Run the project.
Look in the output - you'll see output from the print statement in the Node2D script
```gdscript
extends Node2D

@export var child: Node

func _process(delta):
	print(child) # This prints the child export variable
```
Despite having set the "Child" export to null via clear in the editor, the output shows that it is "Node:<Node#29527901525>" - which is the default value as set in the Node2D scene.

Alternatively, access the node_2d.tscn scene. Set the default variable of the "Child" export variable to null via Clear. Running the project now, the output displays "null" - the expected value.

summary: Implement AI Chat Interface Plugin (Proof of Concept)

description.text:
Adds a new Godot editor plugin module `ai_chat_interface`.

This plugin provides a dockable chat interface within the Godot editor, designed to communicate with an AI backend (e.g., via Cloudflare Workers) over WebSockets.

Key features implemented:
- Basic plugin structure and registration.
- Chat UI with message history, input field, and send button.
- WebSocket client integration for backend communication.
- Core functionalities:
  - Scene Manipulation: Handles a `CREATE_NODE` command from the AI to create new nodes in the edited scene, with undo/redo support.
  - Scripting Assistance: Handles an `INSERT_CODE_SNIPPET` command to insert code into the active script editor, with undo/redo support.
- Project Understanding:
  - Handles a `GET_PROJECT_SETTING` command to retrieve and send project settings to the AI.
  - Handles a `LIST_SCENE_FILES` command to scan and send a list of scene files to the AI.

This implementation serves as a proof of concept for an AI-first game development experience within the Godot editor. Further testing, refinement, and expansion of AI commands are required for a production-ready feature.

summary: Fix GDScript tooltip showing global method overrides with warnings

description.text:
Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/106840

Changes: 
- Fixed GDScript editor tooltip to look for global functions before local methods. 
  - (consistent with GDScript execution behaviour)
  - Overridden global functions now show tooltips in local function declarations. 
- Added warnings when local methods override global functions. 
  - (does not affect named lambdas)
- Added test cases for the new warnings. 

Alternatives: 
- Add a new `GLOBAL_FUNCTION_OVERRIDE` instead of reusing `NATIVE_METHOD_OVERRIDE`?

Note: 
- Related to (but independent of) https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106984. 
- When the cursor hovers over the left edge of a named lambda, i.e. `var x = func ^named_lambda()...`, an unnecessary/incorrect tooltip is shown. 
  - The GDScript parser incorrectly sets `context.type` to `COMPLETION_IDENTIFIER` instead of `COMPLETION_NONE`. 
  - Will be fixed in a future PR. 

My first Draft PR, any help is appreciated!

summary: Fix GDScript tooltip showing shadowed global constants with warnings

description.text:
Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/106944

Changes: 
- Fixed GDScript editor tooltip to look for local variables/ constants/ enums/ functions before global constants. 
  - (consistent with GDScript execution behaviour)
  - `PI`, `TAU`, `INF`, and `NAN` no longer show tooltips in local declarations. 
- Fixed GDScript editor tooltip to look for global methods before local variables. 
  - (consistent with GDScript execution behaviour)
- Added warnings when local variables/ constants/ enums/ functions shadow global constants. 
  - (does not affect named lambdas)
  - (includes `@GDScript` and `@GlobalScope` constants, enums, and flags)
  - (other files also hardcode the check for `PI`, `TAU`, `INF`, and `NAN`; maybe add `is_gdscript_constant()` in a future PR)
- Added test cases for the new warnings. 

Notes: 
- The [Ignore] warning button inserts `@warning_ignore("shadowed_global_identifier")` above/inside enum declarations, which produces parse errors. 
  - A workaround is to surround the enum declaration with @warning_ignore_start("shadowed_global_identifier") + @warning_ignore_restore("shadowed_global_identifier"). 
  - Will be fixed in a future PR. 
- `PI`, `TAU`, `INF`, and `NAN` still have red colour when they are non-global. 
  - They are (intentionally or historically?) coded as keywords, so they are red like other keywords. 
  - Changing this would require a lot more work, because `GDScriptSyntaxHighlighter::_get_line_syntax_highlighting_impl()` does not check if `PI` is global or not (local variables are ignored and have default font colour). 
- Built-in types and classes sometimes produce parse errors instead of being shadowed. 
  - (e.g. `var int = 1`, `var Node = 2`)
  - Unsure what the intended behaviour is, so the related test cases are commented out. 
  - Will be fixed in a future PR. 

My first PR, any help is appreciated!

prints(has_node(extra_arg_0))

summary: Gridmap cursor position is off when half resolution is enabled.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in **4.5.dev4**, **4.4.1.stable**.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6) - Gentoo 2.17 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Single-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon Graphics (radeonsi, renoir, ACO, DRM 3.61, 6.14.6-gentoo) - AMD Ryzen 5 4500U with Radeon Graphics (6 threads)

### Issue description

When half resolution is enabled, the Gridmap cursor is off by what seems to be half the actual mouse position.
The mouse position does not scale when half resolution is enabled and the Gridmap node's cursor is not where the mouse is pointing to in 3D space.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a 3D scene or use an existing one and add a Gridmap node. If you try drawing (if you have an existing meshlibrary) or simply selecting cells with your cursor, it should work as expected, until you enable *Half Resolution* in **the three dotted icon on the top left corner of the viewport** -> **Half Resolution** (at the bottom). Now the Gridmap's cursor isn't where the mouse is pointing to anymore.

summary: Import default for Remove Immutable Tracks ignored

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Windows / 4.4.1 stable, as well as 4.3

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

Turning off Project Settings > Import Defaults > Remove Immutable Tracks should cause newly imported .glb (at least, have not tried other formats) animations to have the Remove Immutable Tracks option turned off, however the Remove Immutable Tracks in newly imported animation is still on, regardless of this setting, and needs to be turned off manually.

(The reason for wanting it off is that it messes up quite a lot of animations)

### Steps to reproduce

- Set Project Settings > Import Defaults > Remove Immutable Tracks to off
- Copy over a .glb animation file somewhere in the project
- The file will be picked up and imported
- Double click the .glb file, and look at the Remove Immutable Tracks flag
- Expectation is it should be off, according to the import default set earlier. It is however on, and needs to be turned off manually


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: get_image() from SubViewport texture is blank.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.1

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6603) - AMD Ryzen 7 3700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

For context, I'm trying to write text on gravestones, so I want to render the gravestone texture with text on top of it to a viewport, use that image as a unique texture for a grave stone, then next frame do another one.  I was able to render text to a texture with the viewport texture, however, changing the text updates all instances, so I tried getting the image off of the texture to make a unique texture (since you can't duplicate() a viewport texture), but the image ended up blank.  I even tried writing it to a file to be sure.



### Steps to reproduce

- Create a scene with a SubViewport node.  Under the viewport node, have a control node with a label/color rect/whatever.
- Call get_texture() on the SubViewport to get the viewport texture.
- (optional) Apply the texture to a material or something and note that the contents of the viewport display correctly.
- Call get_image() on the viewport texture and note that the image is blank -- no text or color rect show up.


```gdscript
extends Node3D

@onready var sub_viewport: SubViewport = $SubViewport
@onready var mesh_nodes: Node3D = $MeshNodes
@onready var label: Label = $SubViewport/TestRenderScene/Label

# Press space to update texture
var mesh_index := 0

func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
	if (Input.is_action_just_pressed("update_texture")):
		print("Updating texture.")
		await RenderingServer.frame_post_draw
		print("Updating texture post draw.")
		var viewport_texture := sub_viewport.get_texture()
		var image := viewport_texture.get_image()
		image.save_png("user://test_image.png")
		var image_texture = ImageTexture.create_from_image(image)
		var mesh : MeshInstance3D = mesh_nodes.get_child(mesh_index)
		var material : StandardMaterial3D = mesh.get_surface_override_material(0)
		material = material.duplicate()
		material.albedo_texture = image_texture
		mesh.set_surface_override_material(0, material)
		mesh_index += 1
		label.text = str(mesh_index, " text")
```

It's entirely possible I'm doing something wrong here, but it seems like this should work.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test_viewport_tombstone_render.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20516099/test_viewport_tombstone_render.zip)

Press space to update 1 mesh.

If you change `material.albedo_texture = image_texture` to `material.albedo_texture = viewport_texture` you can see the meshes update, but all meshes have the same texture instance.  Using the image just has a blank (dark grey?) texture.

summary: Circular Reference Causes Singleton Resource Loading Failure in Godot4.41

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot Version:Reproducible in: 4.4.1 Stable, 4.2.2 Stable




### System information

win11 3050ti

### Issue description

A circular reference in my project causes a bug where a singleton (game_manager) fails to preload a resource in its _ready method. Specifically, the issue occurs when the Bouleuterion node includes a bug3 method and a scene_manager.gd file exists in the project. This setup triggers a failure in resource loading for game_manager.


### Steps to reproduce

Steps to Reproduce:
Open the house or Bouleuterion scene,Run the project. then inspect the game_manager class to observe the resource loading in the _ready method.

Previous Workaround (No Longer Effective):
Previously, I resolved the issue by adding a preload statement in other scenes, such as house or Bouleuterion, for the resource (e.g., preload("res://Asset/tres/bentupai.tres")). This approach worked temporarily but is no longer effective, possibly because I deleted other code that was necessary for the workaround to function. The deleted code may have resolved the circular reference or altered the loading order.

summary: is_hovered() returns inverted state for Button in Godot 4.4.1

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4.1 Stable Official 

### System information

Linux, PopOs

### Issue description

`is_hovered()` on a `Button` returns `false` when the mouse is over the button (after `mouse_entered`) and `true` when not (after `mouse_exited`), opposite of documented behavior.
- Signals (`mouse_entered`, `mouse_exited`) fire correctly.
- Issue is reproducible in a minimal project.


### Steps to reproduce

- Create a new Godot 4.4.1 project.
- Add a Node with a Button child (or create Button via script).
- Attach the provided script.

summary: Specific C# Generic Script setup consistently throws errors from the ScriptManagerBridge

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX TITAN X (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-5820K CPU @ 3.30GHz (12 threads)

### Issue description
Similar to #79519, but the error refers to a different type dictionary a few lines above.

The issue occurs when:
1. You have this script setup:
```c#
public abstract partial class BaseNode : Node {}        // in "BaseNode.cs"

public abstract partial class BaseNode<T> : BaseNode {} // in "GenericNode.cs"

public partial class BoolNode1 : BaseNode<bool> {}      // in "BoolNode1.cs"

public partial class BoolNode2 : BaseNode<bool> {}      // in "BoolNode2.cs"

public partial class MyOtherScript : Node {}            // in "MyOtherScript.cs"
``` 

2. The BaseNode<T> class is in a file that is named differently from the class's name (explained in reproducible steps).
3. You have a specific scene setup (explained in reproducible steps).
4. You rebuild scripts. This will output an error to the console that requires the project to be reloaded.

### Steps to reproduce

### **Step 1**
Create the scripts shown above in their respective files. Ensure that the generic BaseNode is in a file with a name different from "BaseNode.cs." This would, of course, be natural as the BaseNode script assumedly already took that name.

### **Step 2**
Create a scene with three nodes:
1. With BoolNode1 attached
2. With any other script attached
3. With BoolNode2 attached

### **Step 3**
Make sure your scene file (.tscn) has an external resource load order that looks like this:
```
[ext_resource type="Script" uid="uid://mmohqqmb5jr8" path="res://Scripts/NodeTest/BoolNode1.cs" id="1_wicfy"]
[ext_resource type="Script" uid="uid://c7pg1jm2ejbns" path="res://Scripts/TestScripts/MyOtherScript.cs" id="2_go42g"]
[ext_resource type="Script" uid="uid://beynnsmrk5ry2" path="res://Scripts/NodeTest/BoolNode2.cs" id="3_sreuk"]
```

Specifically notice how "MyOtherScript.cs" is being loaded in between the two generic types because it has ID 2. If you have to change this manually, make sure to also change the IDs in the node definitions at the bottom of the file.

### **Step 4**
Reload the project. 

### **Step 5**
Make a change in some C# script so recompiling actually reloads scripts, then press Alt + B to recompile scripts in the editor. This will result in this error in the console:

```
  ERROR: /root/godot/modules/mono/glue/GodotSharp/GodotSharp/Core/NativeInterop/ExceptionUtils.cs:113 - System.ArgumentException: An item with the same key has already been added. Key: BaseNode`1[System.Boolean]
  ERROR:    at System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2.TryInsert(TKey key, TValue value, InsertionBehavior behavior)
  ERROR:    at System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2.Add(TKey key, TValue value)
  ERROR:    at Godot.Bridge.ScriptManagerBridge.ScriptTypeBiMap.Add(IntPtr scriptPtr, Type scriptType) in /root/godot/modules/mono/glue/GodotSharp/GodotSharp/Core/Bridge/ScriptManagerBridge.types.cs:line 30
  ERROR:    at Godot.Bridge.ScriptManagerBridge.TryReloadRegisteredScriptWithClass(IntPtr scriptPtr) in /root/godot/modules/mono/glue/GodotSharp/GodotSharp/Core/Bridge/ScriptManagerBridge.cs:line 686
```

and any following recompilations result in:

```
  ERROR: modules/mono/mono_gd/gd_mono.cpp:673 - .NET: Failed to unload assemblies. Please check https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/78513 for more information.
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Minimal Reproduction Project is here: [Generic-MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20510413/Generic-MRP.zip).

Includes the scene "TestScene.tscn" that already includes the setup described above. To see the error:
1. Open the project.
2. Make a change to some script file (i.e. just make a comment or add a space) so Godot knows to recompile scripts.
3. Recompile with Alt + B while TestScene is open.

summary: Range derived nodes min_value property triggers signal value_changed when changed

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.official.49a5bc7b6

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Linux Mint 21.3 (Virginia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (nvidia; 570.133.07) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12600K (16 threads)

### Issue description

Range derived nodes _min_value_ property triggers the signal _value_changed_ when changed.

As I expected, the _max_value_ property doesn't trigger the value_changed signal when it changes but the _value_ property does.

**I don't expect _min_value_ to trigger the _value_changed_ signal. That's a bug to me.**


The docs says:
● changed()
Emitted when min_value, max_value, page, or step change.

● value_changed(value: float)
Emitted when value changes. When used on a Slider, this is called continuously while dragging (potentially every frame). If you are performing an expensive operation in a function connected to value_changed, consider using a debouncing Timer to call the function less often.

Note: Unlike signals such as LineEdit.text_changed, value_changed is also emitted when value is set directly via code.


What I understand there is:
_changed_ -> Triggered when the properties _min_value_, _max_value_, _page_, or _step_ change.

_value_changed_ -> Triggered when the _value_ property changes. _min_value_ is not stated in the docs under this signal.

### Steps to reproduce

Create an empty project, add a HSlider as the root node, save it as the project's main scene, anchor the node to the center, resize the node.

Attach a script to it:

```
extends Range


func _ready() -> void:
	self.set_min(25.0)
	#self.set_value(10.0)
	#self.set_max(50.0)


func _on_value_changed(new_value: float) -> void:
	print("new_value: ", new_value)

```

Go in the node tab in the editor and connect the signal value_changed.

Run the scene.

summary: Converting TileMap to TileMapLayer adds extraneous Property (use_kinematic_bodies) in .tscn file

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1
- Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS (Focal Fossa) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) UHD Graphics (CML GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10710U CPU @ 1.10GHz (12 threads)

### Issue description

I was re-creating a demo-project manually, in 4.4.1, and created a TileMapLayer (after seeing that TileMap was deprecated), but it didn't work like the demo I was copying. Looking further, the demo-project was 4.3, and I had converted it to 4.4.1, to compare, and discovered some anomalies in the newly node-transformed TileMapLayer. It had the property `use_kinematic_bodies = true` in two places, in the .tscn file, and when I checked/unchecked the Property checkbox in the Editor tab, it removed only one of them. So there was a case where the .tscn file had the property set to True, but in the Editor, it showed as unchecked. (Took me a long time to debug that, comparing my broken project to the working demo-project, too).

```
$:~/Code/test/book_jungle_jump$ grep -rni use_kinematic_bodies .
./moving_platform.tscn:11:use_kinematic_bodies = true
./moving_platform.tscn:17:use_kinematic_bodies = true

```

### Steps to reproduce

Step 0: get initial, unconverted TileMap

    git checkout Step-0-PreConvertTileMap

    - note that there is a TileMap, with "collision_animatable=true".
    - I'm not sure that's required, it seemed to generate the property either way


Step 1: Extract TileMap Layers

either manually do:

    - Select TileMap
    - click toolkit "Extract TileMap layers as individual TileMapLayer nodes"

or

    git checkout Step01-ExtractTileMap-Layers


Step 1 Results:

There is a "use_kinematic_bodies = true" in the .tscn file

	git diff moving_platform.tscn

    +[node name="Layer0" type="TileMapLayer" parent="TileMap"]
    +use_parent_material = true
    +tile_map_data = PackedByteArray(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 15, 0, 14, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 17, 0, 14, 0, 0, 0, 2, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 17, 0, 14, 0, 0, 0, 3, 0, 
    +tile_set = ExtResource("2_iov41")
    +use_kinematic_bodies = true


Step 2: Convert TileMap to TileMapLayer

either manually do:
    - Select TileMap
    - right-click->Change Type->TileMapLayer

or
    git checkout Step02-ConvertTileMap-to-TileMapLayer


Step 2 Results:

There is a "use_kinematic_bodies = true", although in the Editor, the checkbox is not checked.

    $ tail -6 moving_platform.tscn 

    [node name="Layer0" type="TileMapLayer" parent="TileMap"]
    use_parent_material = true
    tile_map_data = PackedByteArray(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 15, 0, 14, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 17, 0, 14, 0, 0, 0, 2, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 17, 0, 14, 0, 0, 0, 3, 0, 0, 
    tile_set = ExtResource("2_iov41")
    use_kinematic_bodies = true


Step 3: Check the box for "use_kinematic_bodies = true", save file


Step 3 Results:

There are two identical lines in the .tscn file, one of which doesn't show up in the Editor

    $ grep -rni use_kinematic_bodies .
    ./moving_platform.tscn:11:use_kinematic_bodies = true
    ./moving_platform.tscn:17:use_kinematic_bodies = true

summary: Fix CodeEdit hover word lookup

description.text:
- Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/102431

Fixes hovering to show tooltip when over the end of the last character of a symbol (where clicking would place the caret at the end). 
This should be useful for cases like https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11365 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/102380, since selecting the entire word puts the caret at the last character.

Unifies the word detection behavior to the one that the rest of the TextEdit uses.
This fixes hovering to show tooltip not working correctly when next to unicode word break characters. Custom word separators are now respected too.
Removed the old word detection function.

Moves the string detection logic to use CodeEdit's delimiter system. Though this means it won't work in comments anymore.

Also made the tooltip hover work when text is selected. There shouldn't be any problem with allowing this, since the tooltip doesn't show if mouse buttons are held. Related: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/discussions/12492

summary: CheckBox's StyleBoxTexture margins not working as expected

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.1.
Not reproducible in 4.2.1.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7270) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

I have a CheckBox with a theme applied. I set all the non-mirrored styles to an empty StyleBoxTexture. If I set left or right margin to 1 in any StyleBox except "normal" ("hover", "pressed", "hover_pressed", "disabled"), it does nothing. The icon isn't shifted and the size of the CheckBox is unchanged. However if I set top or bottom margin the checkbox is resized.
In case of "normal" it works as expected. Changing any of the margins resizes the CheckBox.
This doesn't seem to be the intended behaviour.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a Control node and CheckBox as its child. Create a theme and apply it to the CheckBox.
In the theme editor add CheckBox and then add new StyleBoxTexture to "disabled", "hover", "hover_pressed", "normal", "pressed".
Shrink the CheckBox as much as possible.
